Class OpenCLBackend

java.lang.Object
org.jscience.core.technical.backend.gpu.opencl.OpenCLBackend
All Implemented Interfaces:
Backend, ComputeBackend, GPUBackend
@AutoService({Backend.class,ComputeBackend.class}) public class OpenCLBackend extends Object implements GPUBackend
OpenCL implementation of GPUBackend for cross-platform hardware acceleration.

Uses JOCL to execute computations on GPUs from various vendors (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel).

Since:
1.0
Author:
Silvere Martin-Michiellot, Gemini AI (Google DeepMind)

    Returns information about available GPU devices.

    getId()
    Returns the unique identifier for this backend (e.g., "javafx", "jmol", "cuda").

    getName()
    Returns the display name for UI presentation.
    int
    getPriority()
    Returns the priority for auto-selection (higher = preferred).
    boolean

    Checks if this backend is currently available (libraries loaded, etc.).

    Performs matrix multiplication C = A * B on the GPU.

    • createContext

      public ExecutionContext createContext()
      Description copied from interface: ComputeBackend
      Creates an execution context for running operations.

      The context should be used within a try-with-resources block to ensure proper cleanup of resources.

      Specified by:
      createContext in interface ComputeBackend
      Returns:
      new execution context

    • supportsParallelOps

      public boolean supportsParallelOps()
      Description copied from interface: ComputeBackend
      Checks if this backend supports parallel operations.
      Specified by:
      supportsParallelOps in interface ComputeBackend
      Returns:
      true if parallel execution is supported

    • getPriority

      public int getPriority()
      Description copied from interface: Backend
      Returns the priority for auto-selection (higher = preferred). Used when multiple backends are available.
      Specified by:
      getPriority in interface Backend

    • matrixMultiply

      public void matrixMultiply(DoubleBuffer A, DoubleBuffer B, DoubleBuffer C, int m, int n, int k)
      Description copied from interface: GPUBackend
      Performs matrix multiplication C = A * B on the GPU. Dimensions: A(m x k), B(k x n), C(m x n).
      Specified by:
      matrixMultiply in interface GPUBackend
